---
title: "dia_nn_downstream_analysis"
output: html_document
date: "2026-06-09"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}
library(limpa)
library(readxl)
library(pheatmap)
library(EnhancedVolcano)
library(STRINGdb)
library(clusterProfiler)
library(org.Hs.eg.db)
library(arrow)
library(rpx)
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
```




Answering the bio questions properly 


Reading in the peptides with a q cutoff of 0.01.

```{r}
y.peptide <- readDIANN(file='/Users/mikhaila/Desktop/MSProt2026/team2/transfer_421909_files_47374ab3/report.parquet',
                       format="parquet",
                       q.columns = c("Q.Value","Lib.Q.Value","Lib.PG.Q.Value"),
                       q.cutoffs = 0.01)
```


```{r}
# 1. Build Metadata Table
sample_names <- colnames(y.peptide$E)
samples_stool <- data.frame(File.Name = sample_names) %>%
  mutate(
    Sample_ID = basename(File.Name) %>% str_remove(".raw"),
    Genotype = ifelse(str_detect(Sample_ID, "c-jun_KO"), "c-jun_KO", "CTRL"),
    Treatment = case_when(
      str_detect(Sample_ID, "JNK-IN-8") ~ "JNK-IN-8 + cDDP",
      str_detect(Sample_ID, "cDDP")     ~ "cDDP",
      TRUE                              ~ "Untreated"
    ),
    Timepoint = case_when(
      str_detect(Sample_ID, "06h") ~ "6h",
      str_detect(Sample_ID, "24h") ~ "24h",
      TRUE                         ~ "0h"
    )
  )

# 2. Attach Metadata to limma object
rownames(samples_stool) <- samples_stool$File.Name
y.peptide$targets <- samples_stool[, c("Genotype", "Treatment", "Timepoint")]
```



```{r}


# 1. Base Structural Filters
y.peptide <- filterNonProteotypicPeptides(y.peptide)
y.peptide <- filterCompoundProteins(y.peptide)
y.peptide <- filterSingletonPeptides(y.peptide, min.n.peptides = 2)

# 2. Missing Value Filter (30% Global Rule)
total_samples <- ncol(y.peptide$E)
min_samples <- round(0.30 * total_samples)
valid_values_per_peptide <- rowSums(!is.na(y.peptide$E))

peptides_to_drop <- sum(valid_values_per_peptide < min_samples)
print(paste("Filtering out", peptides_to_drop, "peptides due to too many missing values!"))

y.peptide <- y.peptide[valid_values_per_peptide >= min_samples, ]
```


```{r}
fasta_file <- "/Users/mikhaila/Desktop/MSProt2026/team2/transfer_421909_files_47374ab3/contaminants.fasta"
fasta_lines <- readLines(fasta_file)
headers <- fasta_lines[str_detect(fasta_lines, "^>")]

contam_ids <- headers %>% 
  str_remove("^>") %>% 
  word(1) %>% 
  str_remove("^(sp|tr|CON_)\\|") %>% 
  str_remove("\\|.*$")              

search_pattern <- paste(contam_ids, collapse = "|")
y.peptide$genes <- y.peptide$genes %>% filter(!str_detect(Protein.Group, search_pattern))
y.peptide$E <- y.peptide$E[rownames(y.peptide$genes), ]
```

```{r pca_plot_custom_colors, fig.width=10, fig.height=7}
library(ggplot2)

# Set Output Directory
out_dir <- "/Users/mikhaila/Desktop/MSProt2026/team2/plots_1306"
dir.create(out_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

# 1. Prepare data for PCA (Filtering for complete cases)
pca_matrix <- y.peptide$E
pca_matrix <- pca_matrix[complete.cases(pca_matrix), ]

# 2. Run PCA
pca_res <- prcomp(t(pca_matrix), scale. = TRUE)

# 3. Extract scores and merge with metadata
pca_data <- as.data.frame(pca_res$x)
pca_data$File.Name <- rownames(pca_data)

# Fetch your existing metadata (using your already formatted 'Group' column)
meta_data <- y.peptide$targets
meta_data$File.Name <- rownames(meta_data)

pca_full <- merge(pca_data, meta_data, by="File.Name")

# Calculate variance explained by PC1 and PC2
var_explained <- round(100 * pca_res$sdev^2 / sum(pca_res$sdev^2), 1)

# 4. Define the custom color palette to match your uploaded legend
custom_colors <- c(
  "cjunKO_cDDP_24h"      = "#56C1FF",   
  "cjunKO_cDDP_6h"       = "#ED9A8D",   
  "cjunKO_Untreated_0h"  = "#CF9BFA",   
  "CTRL_cDDP_24h"        = "#A5CA39",   
  "CTRL_cDDP_6h"         = "#F378E0",   
  "CTRL_JNKIN8_cDDP_24h" = "#5CD9D7",   
  "CTRL_JNKIN8_cDDP_6h"  = "#DEAE3B",   
  "CTRL_Untreated_0h"    = "#6CD184"    
)

# 5. Generate the final PCA plot
p_custom <- ggplot(pca_full, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, color = Group)) +
  geom_point(size = 5, alpha = 0.8) +
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_colors) + 
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "PCA of Proteomic Profiles",
    subtitle = "Colored by Experimental Condition",
    x = paste0("PC1 (", var_explained[1], "%)"),
    y = paste0("PC2 (", var_explained[2], "%)"),
    color = "Experimental_Condition"
  ) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12, color = "gray40"),
    legend.position = "right",
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 10)
  )

# 6. Display inline and Save to SVG
print(p_custom)
ggsave(filename = file.path(out_dir, "PCA_Plot_Combined.svg"), plot = p_custom, width = 10, height = 7)
```


```{r}
# 1. Create a combined Group variable and clean out characters that break R variable names (- and +)
y.peptide$targets$Group <- paste(y.peptide$targets$Genotype, 
                                 y.peptide$targets$Treatment, 
                                 y.peptide$targets$Timepoint, 
                                 sep = "_") %>%
  str_replace_all("c-jun_KO", "cjunKO") %>%
  str_replace_all("JNK-IN-8 \\+ cDDP", "JNKIN8_cDDP")

# 2. Build the Zero-Intercept Design Matrix
Group <- factor(y.peptide$targets$Group)
design <- model.matrix(~ 0 + Group)
colnames(design) <- levels(Group)

# 3. Fit the base model
fit <- lmFit(y.peptide$E, design)

# 4. Build the Comparisons using the clean, valid column names
contrasts_matrix <- makeContrasts(
  Mechanistic_Baseline    = "cjunKO_Untreated_0h - CTRL_Untreated_0h",
  Mechanistic_Stress      = "cjunKO_cDDP_24h - CTRL_cDDP_24h",
  Mechanistic_Stress_1 = "cjunKO_cDDP_6h - CTRL_cDDP_6h", 
  Translational_Inhibitor = "CTRL_JNKIN8_cDDP_24h - CTRL_cDDP_24h",
  Translational_Inhibitor_1 = "CTRL_JNKIN8_cDDP_6h - CTRL_cDDP_6h",
  Chemical_Genetic = "CTRL_JNKIN8_cDDP_24h - cjunKO_cDDP_24h",
  Chemical_Genetic_1 = "CTRL_JNKIN8_cDDP_6h - cjunKO_cDDP_6h",
  Biomarker_Resistance    = "CTRL_cDDP_24h - CTRL_Untreated_0h",
  levels = design
)

# 5. Calculate final statistics
fit_contrasts <- contrasts.fit(fit, contrasts_matrix)
fit_contrasts = eBayes(fit_contrasts)
```
```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
library(EnhancedVolcano)
library(pheatmap)

# ==============================================================================
# 1. PARSE DATA & CREATE METADATA (FIXING THE TIMEPOINT NOMENCLATURE)
# ==============================================================================

# Extract base file labels from the DIANN matrix
sample_names <- colnames(y.peptide$E)

samples_stool <- data.frame(File.Name = sample_names) %>%
  mutate(
    Sample_ID = basename(File.Name) %>% str_remove(".raw"),
    Genotype = ifelse(str_detect(Sample_ID, "c-jun_KO"), "c-jun_KO", "CTRL"),
    Treatment = case_when(
      str_detect(Sample_ID, "JNK-IN-8") ~ "JNK-IN-8 + cDDP",
      str_detect(Sample_ID, "cDDP")     ~ "cDDP",
      TRUE                              ~ "Untreated"
    ),
    # FIXED: Hardcodes '6h' instead of '06h' to align perfectly with your contrast matrix strings
    Timepoint = case_when(
      str_detect(Sample_ID, "06h") ~ "6h", 
      str_detect(Sample_ID, "24h") ~ "24h",
      TRUE                         ~ "0h"
    )
  )

# Set matrix matching indices 
rownames(samples_stool) <- samples_stool$File.Name
y.peptide$targets <- samples_stool[, c("Genotype", "Treatment", "Timepoint")]

# ==============================================================================
# 2. CONSTRUCT STRIPPED GROUP LABELS & MODEL FIT
# ==============================================================================

y.peptide$targets$Group <- paste(y.peptide$targets$Genotype, 
                                 y.peptide$targets$Treatment, 
                                 y.peptide$targets$Timepoint, 
                                 sep = "_") %>%
  str_replace_all("c-jun_KO", "cjunKO") %>%
  str_replace_all("JNK-IN-8 \\+ cDDP", "JNKIN8_cDDP")

# Structural fitting matrices
Group <- factor(y.peptide$targets$Group)
design <- model.matrix(~ 0 + Group)
colnames(design) <- levels(Group)

fit <- lmFit(y.peptide$E, design)

contrasts_matrix <- makeContrasts(
  Mechanistic_Baseline      = "cjunKO_Untreated_0h - CTRL_Untreated_0h",
  Mechanistic_Stress        = "cjunKO_cDDP_24h - CTRL_cDDP_24h",
  Mechanistic_Stress_1      = "cjunKO_cDDP_6h - CTRL_cDDP_6h", 
  Translational_Inhibitor   = "CTRL_JNKIN8_cDDP_24h - CTRL_cDDP_24h",
  Translational_Inhibitor_1 = "CTRL_JNKIN8_cDDP_6h - CTRL_cDDP_6h",
  Chemical_Genetic          = "CTRL_JNKIN8_cDDP_24h - cjunKO_cDDP_24h",
  Chemical_Genetic_1        = "CTRL_JNKIN8_cDDP_6h - cjunKO_cDDP_6h",
  Biomarker_Resistance      = "CTRL_cDDP_24h - CTRL_Untreated_0h",
  levels = design
)

fit_contrasts <- contrasts.fit(fit, contrasts_matrix)
fit_contrasts <- eBayes(fit_contrasts)

# ==============================================================================
# 3. DYNAMIC GRAPHING ENGINE FOR ALL CONTRASTS
# ==============================================================================
library(grid) # Required to draw pheatmaps inline during a loop

out_dir <- "/Users/mikhaila/Desktop/MSProt2026/team2/plots_1306"
contrast_names <- colnames(fit_contrasts$contrasts)

for (contrast in contrast_names) {
  
  cat("\n\n### Contrast Group:", contrast, "\n")
  
  # ==========================================
  # VOLCANO PLOT
  # ==========================================
  res <- topTable(fit_contrasts, coef = contrast, number = Inf, adjust.method = "BH")
  intersecting_ids <- intersect(rownames(res), rownames(y.peptide$genes))
  res <- res[intersecting_ids, ]
  
  res$Genes <- y.peptide$genes[intersecting_ids, "Genes"]
  res$Protein.Group <- y.peptide$genes[intersecting_ids, "Protein.Group"]
  
  res$PlotLabel <- ifelse(is.na(res$Genes) | res$Genes == "" | res$Genes == "NA", 
                          res$Protein.Group, res$Genes)
  
  duplicate_mask <- duplicated(res$PlotLabel)
  res$PlotLabel[duplicate_mask] <- ""
  
  to_label <- res[res$adj.P.Val < 0.01 & abs(res$logFC) > 1 & res$PlotLabel != "", ]
  
  if (nrow(to_label) > 0) {
    to_label <- to_label[order(to_label$adj.P.Val / abs(to_label$logFC)), ]
    top_15_custom_genes <- head(to_label$PlotLabel, 15)
  } else {
    fallback_res <- res[res$PlotLabel != "", ]
    fallback_res <- fallback_res[order(fallback_res$adj.P.Val / abs(fallback_res$logFC)), ]
    top_15_custom_genes <- head(fallback_res$PlotLabel, 15)
  }
  
  v_plot <- EnhancedVolcano(res,
                            lab = res$PlotLabel,
                            selectLab = top_15_custom_genes, 
                            x = 'logFC', 
                            y = 'adj.P.Val',
                            pCutoff = 0.01, FCcutoff = 1.0, 
                            pointSize = 1.8, labSize = 3.0,
                            drawConnectors = TRUE, widthConnectors = 0.4,
                            colConnectors = 'grey40', max.overlaps = 50,
                            title = paste("Volcano Dynamics:", str_replace_all(contrast, "_", " ")),
                            subtitle = "Top 15 unique genes",
                            legendPosition = 'bottom')
  
  # Print Volcano Inline & Save SVG
  print(v_plot)
  ggsave(filename = file.path(out_dir, paste0("Volcano_", contrast, ".svg")), plot = v_plot, width = 9, height = 8)

  # ==========================================
  # HEATMAP
  # ==========================================
  non_na_peptides <- rownames(y.peptide$E)[rowSums(is.na(y.peptide$E)) == 0]
  res <- res[rownames(res) %in% non_na_peptides, ]
  
  res_unique <- res %>% distinct(PlotLabel, .keep_all = TRUE)
  
  sig_features <- res_unique %>% filter(adj.P.Val < 0.05) %>% head(50)
  if(nrow(sig_features) < 15) { sig_features <- head(res_unique, 50) }
  
  if (nrow(sig_features) > 5) {
    heatmap_matrix <- y.peptide$E[rownames(sig_features), , drop = FALSE]
    rownames(heatmap_matrix) <- sig_features$PlotLabel
    
    annotation_col <- data.frame(Experimental_Condition = factor(y.peptide$targets$Group))
    rownames(annotation_col) <- colnames(y.peptide$E)
    
    # Setup custom colors array for pheatmap metadata matching the PCA
    ann_colors = list(Experimental_Condition = custom_colors)
    
    ph <- pheatmap(heatmap_matrix,
                   scale = "row", 
                   clustering_distance_rows = "correlation",
                   clustering_distance_cols = "euclidean",
                   clustering_method = "ward.D2",
                   annotation_col = annotation_col,
                   annotation_colors = ann_colors,  # Colors synced with PCA
                   show_colnames = FALSE,
                   fontsize_row = 7,
                   main = paste("Clustering Trends:", str_replace_all(contrast, "_", " ")),
                   silent = TRUE) # Suppress default auto-print so we can control it
    
    # 1. Print inline for the RMarkdown document using grid
    grid.newpage()
    grid.draw(ph$gtable)
    
    # 2. Save explicitly to SVG using the base graphics device
    svg(filename = file.path(out_dir, paste0("Heatmap_", contrast, ".svg")), width = 10, height = 9)
    grid.draw(ph$gtable)
    invisible(dev.off())
    
  } else {
    cat("\nSkipping heatmap generation for", contrast, "— insufficient complete-data features.\n")
  }
}


```
```{r}
library(clusterProfiler)
library(org.Hs.eg.db)

out_dir <- "/Users/mikhaila/Desktop/MSProt2026/team2/plots_1306"
all_contrasts <- colnames(fit_contrasts$contrasts)

for (contrast_name in all_contrasts) {
  
  cat("\n### Pathway Enrichment:", contrast_name, "\n")
  
  res <- topTable(fit_contrasts, coef = contrast_name, number = Inf, adjust.method = "BH")
  intersecting_ids <- intersect(rownames(res), rownames(y.peptide$genes))
  res <- res[intersecting_ids, ]
  res$Protein.Group <- y.peptide$genes[intersecting_ids, "Protein.Group"]
  
  sig_proteins <- res %>% 
    filter(adj.P.Val < 0.05) %>% 
    distinct(Protein.Group, .keep_all = TRUE)
  
  if (nrow(sig_proteins) > 0) {
    conv <- bitr(sig_proteins$Protein.Group, fromType = "UNIPROT", toType = "ENTREZID", OrgDb = org.Hs.eg.db)
    
    if (nrow(conv) > 0) {
      
      # GO Enrichment
      ego <- enrichGO(gene = conv$ENTREZID, OrgDb = org.Hs.eg.db, ont = "ALL", pAdjustMethod = "BH", pvalueCutoff = 0.05, readable = TRUE)
      
      if (!is.null(ego) && nrow(as.data.frame(ego)) > 0) {
        p_go <- dotplot(ego, showCategory = 10, title = paste("GO Enrichment:", str_replace_all(contrast_name, "_", " ")))
        print(p_go)
        ggsave(filename = file.path(out_dir, paste0("GO_", contrast_name, ".svg")), plot = p_go, width = 10, height = 8)
      }
      
      # KEGG Enrichment
      ekegg <- enrichKEGG(gene = conv$ENTREZID, organism = 'hsa', pvalueCutoff = 0.05)
      
      if (!is.null(ekegg) && nrow(as.data.frame(ekegg)) > 0) {
        p_kegg <- dotplot(ekegg, showCategory = 10, title = paste("KEGG Enrichment:", str_replace_all(contrast_name, "_", " ")))
        print(p_kegg)
        ggsave(filename = file.path(out_dir, paste0("KEGG_", contrast_name, ".svg")), plot = p_kegg, width = 10, height = 8)
      }
      
    }
  } else {
    cat("No significant proteins (adj.P.Val < 0.05) found for this contrast.\n")
  }
}
```

```{r pathway_enrichment_all, fig.width=10, fig.height=8, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}

library(clusterProfiler)
library(org.Hs.eg.db)
library(stringr) # make sure stringr is loaded for str_replace_all

out_dir <- "/Users/mikhaila/Desktop/MSProt2026/team2/plots_1306"
dir.create(out_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE) # Safety check to ensure folder exists

all_contrasts <- colnames(fit_contrasts$contrasts)

for (contrast_name in all_contrasts) {
  
  cat("\n### Pathway Enrichment:", contrast_name, "\n")
  
  res <- topTable(fit_contrasts, coef = contrast_name, number = Inf, adjust.method = "BH")
  intersecting_ids <- intersect(rownames(res), rownames(y.peptide$genes))
  res <- res[intersecting_ids, ]
  res$Protein.Group <- y.peptide$genes[intersecting_ids, "Protein.Group"]
  
  sig_proteins <- res %>% 
    filter(adj.P.Val < 0.05) %>% 
    distinct(Protein.Group, .keep_all = TRUE)
  
  if (nrow(sig_proteins) > 0) {
    conv <- bitr(sig_proteins$Protein.Group, fromType = "UNIPROT", toType = "ENTREZID", OrgDb = org.Hs.eg.db)
    
    if (nrow(conv) > 0) {
      
      # ===============================================
      # GO Enrichment
      # ===============================================
      ego <- enrichGO(gene = conv$ENTREZID, OrgDb = org.Hs.eg.db, ont = "ALL", pAdjustMethod = "BH", pvalueCutoff = 0.05, readable = TRUE)
      
      if (!is.null(ego) && nrow(as.data.frame(ego)) > 0) {
        p_go <- dotplot(ego, showCategory = 10, title = paste("GO Enrichment:", str_replace_all(contrast_name, "_", " ")))
        
        # 1. Print inline for the Markdown document
        print(p_go)
        
        # 2. Save explicitly as PDF
        pdf(file = file.path(out_dir, paste0("GO_", contrast_name, ".pdf")), width = 10, height = 8)
        print(p_go)
        invisible(dev.off()) # Safely closes the PDF file
        
        # 3. Save explicitly as SVG
        svg(file = file.path(out_dir, paste0("GO_", contrast_name, ".svg")), width = 10, height = 8)
        print(p_go)
        invisible(dev.off()) # Safely closes the SVG file
      }
      
      # ===============================================
      # KEGG Enrichment
      # ===============================================
      ekegg <- enrichKEGG(gene = conv$ENTREZID, organism = 'hsa', pvalueCutoff = 0.05)
      
      if (!is.null(ekegg) && nrow(as.data.frame(ekegg)) > 0) {
        p_kegg <- dotplot(ekegg, showCategory = 10, title = paste("KEGG Enrichment:", str_replace_all(contrast_name, "_", " ")))
        
        # 1. Print inline for the Markdown document
        print(p_kegg)
        
        # 2. Save explicitly as PDF
        pdf(file = file.path(out_dir, paste0("KEGG_", contrast_name, ".pdf")), width = 10, height = 8)
        print(p_kegg)
        invisible(dev.off()) # Safely closes the PDF file
        
        # 3. Save explicitly as SVG
        svg(file = file.path(out_dir, paste0("KEGG_", contrast_name, ".svg")), width = 10, height = 8)
        print(p_kegg)
        invisible(dev.off()) # Safely closes the SVG file
      }
      
    }
  } else {
    cat("No significant proteins (adj.P.Val < 0.05) found for this contrast.\n")
  }
}
```



Translational quesion: can pharmacological inhibitor mimic the knock out of the cJun 
VENN diagrams and DGE

```{r venn_diagrams, fig.width=8, fig.height=6, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(ggVennDiagram)
library(ggplot2)

out_dir <- "/Users/mikhaila/Desktop/MSProt2026/team2/plots_1306"

# Helper function to extract unique significant gene symbols for a contrast
get_sig_genes <- function(contrast_name) {
  res <- topTable(fit_contrasts, coef = contrast_name, number = Inf, adjust.method = "BH")
  res <- res[res$adj.P.Val < 0.05, ] # Strict significance cutoff
  intersecting_ids <- intersect(rownames(res), rownames(y.peptide$genes))
  genes <- y.peptide$genes[intersecting_ids, "Genes"]
  unique(genes[!is.na(genes) & genes != ""])
}

# 1. Extract the gene lists
genes_KO_6h   <- get_sig_genes("Mechanistic_Stress_1")
genes_Chem_6h <- get_sig_genes("Translational_Inhibitor_1")

genes_KO_24h   <- get_sig_genes("Mechanistic_Stress")
genes_Chem_24h <- get_sig_genes("Translational_Inhibitor")

# 2. Build the lists for Venn plotting
list_6h <- list(`c-Jun KO (6h)` = genes_KO_6h, `JNK-IN-8 (6h)` = genes_Chem_6h)
list_24h <- list(`c-Jun KO (24h)` = genes_KO_24h, `JNK-IN-8 (24h)` = genes_Chem_24h)

# 3. Generate 6h Venn Diagram
venn_6h <- ggVennDiagram(list_6h, label_alpha = 0) +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "#F4FAFE", high = "#56C1FF") +
  theme(legend.position = "none") +
  labs(title = "Gene Overlap at 6h: Genetic KO vs Chemical Inhibition")

print(venn_6h)
ggsave(filename = file.path(out_dir, "Venn_Overlap_6h.svg"), plot = venn_6h, width = 7, height = 5)

# 4. Generate 24h Venn Diagram
venn_24h <- ggVennDiagram(list_24h, label_alpha = 0) +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "#F4FAFE", high = "#A5CA39") +
  theme(legend.position = "none") +
  labs(title = "Gene Overlap at 24h: Genetic KO vs Chemical Inhibition")

print(venn_24h)
ggsave(filename = file.path(out_dir, "Venn_Overlap_24h.svg"), plot = venn_24h, width = 7, height = 5)
```


```{r venn_diagrams, fig.width=8, fig.height=6, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
### 2. Pathway Intersection (CompareCluster)


library(clusterProfiler)
library(org.Hs.eg.db)

# Helper function to convert a list of gene symbols to ENTREZ IDs
convert_to_entrez <- function(gene_symbols) {
  if(length(gene_symbols) == 0) return(character(0))
  conv <- bitr(gene_symbols, fromType = "SYMBOL", toType = "ENTREZID", OrgDb = org.Hs.eg.db)
  return(conv$ENTREZID)
}

# 1. Convert all our significant gene lists to Entrez IDs
entrez_lists <- list(
  `KO 6h` = convert_to_entrez(genes_KO_6h),
  `Chem 6h` = convert_to_entrez(genes_Chem_6h),
  `KO 24h` = convert_to_entrez(genes_KO_24h),
  `Chem 24h` = convert_to_entrez(genes_Chem_24h)
)

# Remove empty lists (in case one contrast yielded 0 genes)
entrez_lists <- entrez_lists[lengths(entrez_lists) > 0]

# 2. Run Biological Intersection for GO Pathways
comp_GO <- compareCluster(geneCluster = entrez_lists, fun = "enrichGO", OrgDb = org.Hs.eg.db, ont = "BP", pvalueCutoff = 0.05)

if (!is.null(comp_GO)) {
  p_comp_GO <- dotplot(comp_GO, showCategory = 8, title = "Pathway Intersection: Genetic vs Chemical (GO Biological Process)")
  print(p_comp_GO)
  ggsave(filename = file.path(out_dir, "Pathway_Intersection_GO.svg"), plot = p_comp_GO, width = 11, height = 8)
} else {
  cat("Not enough overlapping pathways to generate GO intersection plot.\n")
}

# 3. Run Biological Intersection for KEGG Pathways
comp_KEGG <- compareCluster(geneCluster = entrez_lists, fun = "enrichKEGG", organism = "hsa", pvalueCutoff = 0.05)

if (!is.null(comp_KEGG)) {
  p_comp_KEGG <- dotplot(comp_KEGG, showCategory = 8, title = "Pathway Intersection: Genetic vs Chemical (KEGG)")
  print(p_comp_KEGG)
  ggsave(filename = file.path(out_dir, "Pathway_Intersection_KEGG.svg"), plot = p_comp_KEGG, width = 11, height = 8)
} else {
  cat("Not enough overlapping pathways to generate KEGG intersection plot.\n")
}
```
```{r}

library(clusterProfiler)
library(org.Hs.eg.db)
library(ggplot2)

out_dir <- "/Users/mikhaila/Desktop/MSProt2026/team2/plots_1306"

# Helper function to extract significant gene symbols (Using STRICT 0.01 cutoff)
get_sig_genes_strict <- function(contrast_name) {
  res <- topTable(fit_contrasts, coef = contrast_name, number = Inf, adjust.method = "BH")
  res <- res[res$adj.P.Val < 0.01, ] # Strict 0.01 cutoff for peptides/genes
  intersecting_ids <- intersect(rownames(res), rownames(y.peptide$genes))
  genes <- y.peptide$genes[intersecting_ids, "Genes"]
  unique(genes[!is.na(genes) & genes != ""])
}

# 1. Extract the strict gene lists
genes_KO_6h_strict    <- get_sig_genes_strict("Mechanistic_Stress_1")
genes_Chem_6h_strict  <- get_sig_genes_strict("Translational_Inhibitor_1")
genes_KO_24h_strict   <- get_sig_genes_strict("Mechanistic_Stress")
genes_Chem_24h_strict <- get_sig_genes_strict("Translational_Inhibitor")

# Helper function to convert a list of gene symbols to ENTREZ IDs
convert_to_entrez <- function(gene_symbols) {
  if(length(gene_symbols) == 0) return(character(0))
  conv <- bitr(gene_symbols, fromType = "SYMBOL", toType = "ENTREZID", OrgDb = org.Hs.eg.db)
  return(conv$ENTREZID)
}

# 2. Convert all to Entrez
entrez_lists_strict <- list(
  `KO 6h`   = convert_to_entrez(genes_KO_6h_strict),
  `Chem 6h` = convert_to_entrez(genes_Chem_6h_strict),
  `KO 24h`  = convert_to_entrez(genes_KO_24h_strict),
  `Chem 24h`= convert_to_entrez(genes_Chem_24h_strict)
)

# Remove empty lists (in case the strict cutoff eliminates an entire group)
entrez_lists_strict <- entrez_lists_strict[lengths(entrez_lists_strict) > 0]

# ==========================================
# 3. GO Intersection Plot (0.01 Cutoff)
# ==========================================
comp_GO_strict <- compareCluster(geneCluster = entrez_lists_strict, fun = "enrichGO", OrgDb = org.Hs.eg.db, ont = "BP", pvalueCutoff = 0.01)

if (!is.null(comp_GO_strict) && nrow(as.data.frame(comp_GO_strict)) > 0) {
  p_comp_GO_strict <- dotplot(comp_GO_strict, 
                              showCategory = 8, 
                              label_format = 40, 
                              font.size = 10,    
                              title = "Pathway Intersection: Genetic vs Chemical (GO) - FDR < 0.01") +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) 
  
  print(p_comp_GO_strict)
  ggsave(filename = file.path(out_dir, "Pathway_Intersection_GO_0.01.svg"), plot = p_comp_GO_strict, width = 10, height = 10)
} else {
  cat("\nNo GO pathways passed the strict 0.01 cutoff.\n")
}

# ==========================================
# 4. KEGG Intersection Plot (0.01 Cutoff)
# ==========================================
comp_KEGG_strict <- compareCluster(geneCluster = entrez_lists_strict, fun = "enrichKEGG", organism = "hsa", pvalueCutoff = 0.01)

if (!is.null(comp_KEGG_strict) && nrow(as.data.frame(comp_KEGG_strict)) > 0) {
  p_comp_KEGG_strict <- dotplot(comp_KEGG_strict, 
                                showCategory = 8, 
                                label_format = 40, 
                                font.size = 10,    
                                title = "Pathway Intersection: Genetic vs Chemical (KEGG) - FDR < 0.01") +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) 
  
  print(p_comp_KEGG_strict)
  ggsave(filename = file.path(out_dir, "Pathway_Intersection_KEGG_0.01.svg"), plot = p_comp_KEGG_strict, width = 10, height = 10)
} else {
  cat("\nNo KEGG pathways passed the strict 0.01 cutoff.\n")
}
```



```{r direct_comparison_ko_vs_chem, fig.width=10, fig.height=8, warning=FALSE}

library(EnhancedVolcano)

out_dir <- "/Users/mikhaila/Desktop/MSProt2026/team2/plots_1306"

direct_contrasts <- c("Chemical_Genetic_1", "Chemical_Genetic")
time_labels <- c("6h", "24h")

for (i in 1:2) {
  contrast <- direct_contrasts[i]
  time_label <- time_labels[i]
  
  # Fetch data
  res <- topTable(fit_contrasts, coef = contrast, number = Inf, adjust.method = "BH")
  intersecting_ids <- intersect(rownames(res), rownames(y.peptide$genes))
  res <- res[intersecting_ids, ]
  
  res$Genes <- y.peptide$genes[intersecting_ids, "Genes"]
  res$Protein.Group <- y.peptide$genes[intersecting_ids, "Protein.Group"]
  res$PlotLabel <- ifelse(is.na(res$Genes) | res$Genes == "", res$Protein.Group, res$Genes)
  
  # Calculate how many genes are ACTUALLY different between the two
  num_diff <- sum(res$adj.P.Val < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE)
  
  cat("\n======================================================\n")
  cat("Direct Comparison at", time_label, "\n")
  cat("Number of significantly different peptides (adj.P.Val < 0.05):", num_diff, "\n")
  cat("======================================================\n")
  
  # Handle labels safely
  duplicate_mask <- duplicated(res$PlotLabel)
  res$PlotLabel[duplicate_mask] <- ""
  
  sig_hits <- res[res$adj.P.Val < 0.01 & abs(res$logFC) > 1 & res$PlotLabel != "", ]
  if (nrow(sig_hits) > 0) {
    top_labels <- head(sig_hits[order(sig_hits$adj.P.Val), "PlotLabel"], 15)
  } else {
    top_labels <- head(res[order(res$adj.P.Val), "PlotLabel"], 5) # Fallback to top 5 if highly similar
  }

  # Generate Plot
  v_plot <- EnhancedVolcano(res,
                            lab = res$PlotLabel,
                            selectLab = top_labels,
                            x = 'logFC', 
                            y = 'adj.P.Val',
                            pCutoff = 0.05, FCcutoff = 1.0, 
                            pointSize = 2.0, labSize = 4.0,
                            drawConnectors = TRUE, widthConnectors = 0.5,
                            title = paste("Direct Comparison:", time_label),
                            subtitle = "JNK-IN-8 + cDDP  vs  c-Jun KO + cDDP",
                            caption = paste(num_diff, "peptides show significant differences"),
                            legendPosition = 'bottom')
  
  print(v_plot)
  ggsave(filename = file.path(out_dir, paste0("Volcano_Direct_Comparison_", time_label, ".svg")), plot = v_plot, width = 9, height = 8)
}
```



Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.
